(formerly **Flatbush Avenue**) is the westernmost [commuter rail](/wiki/Commuter_rail "Commuter rail") terminal on the [Long Island Rail Road](/wiki/Long_Island_Rail_Road "Long Island Rail Road")'s (LIRR) [Atlantic Branch](/wiki/Atlantic_Branch "Atlantic Branch"), located at [Flatbush Avenue](/wiki/Flatbush_Avenue "Flatbush Avenue") and [Atlantic Avenue](/wiki/Atlantic_Avenue_(New_York_City) "Atlantic Avenue (New York City)") in [Downtown Brooklyn](/wiki/Downtown_Brooklyn "Downtown Brooklyn"), [New York City](/wiki/New_York_City "New York City"). It is the primary terminal for the [West Hempstead Branch](/wiki/West_Hempstead_Branch "West Hempstead Branch"), and a peak-hour terminal for some trains on the [Hempstead Branch](/wiki/Hempstead_Branch "Hempstead Branch"), [Far Rockaway Branch](/wiki/Far_Rockaway_Branch "Far Rockaway Branch"), [Port Jefferson Branch](/wiki/Port_Jefferson_Branch "Port Jefferson Branch"), [Ronkonkoma Branch](/wiki/Ronkonkoma_Branch "Ronkonkoma Branch"), and the [Babylon Branch](/wiki/Babylon_Branch "Babylon Branch");[[2]](#cite_note-MTA_2023_z608-2) most other service is provided by frequent shuttles to [Jamaica station](/wiki/Jamaica_station_(LIRR) "Jamaica station (LIRR)"). The terminal is located in the [City Terminal Zone](/wiki/City_Terminal_Zone "City Terminal Zone"), the LIRR's Zone 1, and thus part of the CityTicket program.

**Atlantic Terminal** (formerly **Flatbush Avenue**) is the westernmost [commuter rail](/wiki/Commuter_rail "Commuter rail") terminal on the [Long Island Rail Road](/wiki/Long_Island_Rail_Road "Long Island Rail Road")'s (LIRR) [Atlantic Branch](/wiki/Atlantic_Branch "Atlantic Branch"), located at [Flatbush Avenue](/wiki/Flatbush_Avenue "Flatbush Avenue") and [Atlantic Avenue](/wiki/Atlantic_Avenue_(New_York_City) "Atlantic Avenue (New York City)") in [Downtown Brooklyn](/wiki/Downtown_Brooklyn "Downtown Brooklyn"), [New York City](/wiki/New_York_City "New York City"). It is the primary terminal for the [West Hempstead Branch](/wiki/West_Hempstead_Branch "West Hempstead Branch"), and a peak-hour terminal for some trains on the [Hempstead Branch](/wiki/Hempstead_Branch "Hempstead Branch"), [Far Rockaway Branch](/wiki/Far_Rockaway_Branch "Far Rockaway Branch"), [Port Jefferson Branch](/wiki/Port_Jefferson_Branch "Port Jefferson Branch"), [Ronkonkoma Branch](/wiki/Ronkonkoma_Branch "Ronkonkoma Branch"), and the [Babylon Branch](/wiki/Babylon_Branch "Babylon Branch");[[2]](#cite_note-MTA_2023_z608-2) most other service is provided by frequent shuttles to [Jamaica station](/wiki/Jamaica_station_(LIRR) "Jamaica station (LIRR)"). The terminal is located in the [City Terminal Zone](/wiki/City_Terminal_Zone "City Terminal Zone"), the LIRR's Zone 1, and thus part of the CityTicket program.

## History

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Atlantic_Terminal&action=edit&section=1 "Edit section: History")]

### 19th century

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Atlantic_Terminal&action=edit&section=2 "Edit section: 19th century")]

The station was originally named *Brooklyn* in 1852,[[3]](#cite_note-trainsarefun.com-3) twenty years after the line was established as the [Brooklyn and Jamaica Railroad](/wiki/Brooklyn_and_Jamaica_Railroad "Brooklyn and Jamaica Railroad"), and was not originally a terminus.[[3]](#cite_note-trainsarefun.com-3) The original terminus was [South Ferry](/wiki/South_Ferry,_Brooklyn "South Ferry, Brooklyn"), via the now shuttered [Cobble Hill Tunnel](/wiki/Cobble_Hill_Tunnel "Cobble Hill Tunnel"). When LIRR subsidiary [New York and Jamaica Railroad](/wiki/New_York_and_Jamaica_Railroad "New York and Jamaica Railroad") built a new line between Hunter's Point and Jamaica in 1861, the main line was relocated there, and the line was abandoned west of [East New York](/wiki/East_New_York,_Brooklyn "East New York, Brooklyn"), in compliance with Brooklyn's ban on steam railroads. West of East New York, the tracks were taken over by horse car lines.

The Brooklyn station designation was replaced by the *Flatbush Avenue* station on July 2, 1877. That same summer local Atlantic Avenue rapid transit trains began to stop there on August 13.[[4]](#cite_note-4) The old depot was renovated between July–August 1878, when it began serving the [Brooklyn, Flatbush and Coney Island Railroad](/wiki/Brooklyn,_Flatbush_and_Coney_Island_Railroad "Brooklyn, Flatbush and Coney Island Railroad"). It was rebuilt again in June 1880. The headquarters for the Long Island Express Company was installed there in 1882, and gave the station a series of tracks that would later be known as the "EX Yard." In 1888, the Union Elevated Railway built an elevated railway line and station that connected to the LIRR station called the [Atlantic Avenue station](/wiki/Atlantic_Avenue_(BMT_Fifth_Avenue_Line) "Atlantic Avenue (BMT Fifth Avenue Line)"). The Union Elevated eventually became part of the [Brooklyn-Manhattan Transit Corporation](/wiki/Brooklyn-Manhattan_Transit_Corporation "Brooklyn-Manhattan Transit Corporation"). Further rebuilding took place again in 1893.

### 20th century

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Atlantic_Terminal&action=edit&section=3 "Edit section: 20th century")]

Between 1904 and 1906, the Carlton Avenue Freight Yards were replaced by the [Vanderbilt Avenue Freight Yards](/wiki/Vanderbilt_Avenue_Freight_Yards "Vanderbilt Avenue Freight Yards"). This was just a portion of a major improvement project that included the complete reconstruction of the station. The second depot opened on April 1, 1907, with the depot at street level and the tracks installed underground. The station had a lobby that was larger than most LIRR stations, and contained subway type entrances to the tracks.[[5]](#cite_note-5) It also served as a post office building until 1925,[[6]](#cite_note-6) and contained a baggage depot, express buildings, some meat houses which were inherited from the previous version of the station, and a merchandise terminal for "less than carload freight" added on in 1908. The [Interborough Rapid Transit Company](/wiki/Interborough_Rapid_Transit_Company "Interborough Rapid Transit Company") built a subway line called the [Eastern Parkway Line](/wiki/IRT_Eastern_Parkway_Line "IRT Eastern Parkway Line") and a [station on Atlantic Avenue](/wiki/Atlantic_Avenue_(IRT_Eastern_Parkway_Line) "Atlantic Avenue (IRT Eastern Parkway Line)"), that connected to the station on May 1, 1908. The BMT also built two more subway lines on [Pacific Street](/wiki/Pacific_Street_(BMT_Fourth_Avenue_Line) "Pacific Street (BMT Fourth Avenue Line)") along the [Fourth Avenue Line](/wiki/BMT_Fourth_Avenue_Line "BMT Fourth Avenue Line") on June 22, 1915, and [Atlantic Avenue](/wiki/Atlantic_Avenue_(BMT_Brighton_Line) "Atlantic Avenue (BMT Brighton Line)") along the [Brighton Line](/wiki/BMT_Brighton_Line "BMT Brighton Line") on August 1, 1920. The connection to the [BMT Fifth Avenue Line](/wiki/BMT_Fifth_Avenue_Line "BMT Fifth Avenue Line") was lost on May 31, 1940.[[7]](#cite_note-7)

[![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/b/b4/Long_Island_Railroad_Station_interior%2C_Flatbush_Avenue_ca._1893.jpg/250px-Long_Island_Railroad_Station_interior%2C_Flatbush_Avenue_ca._1893.jpg)](/wiki/File:Long_Island_Railroad_Station_interior,_Flatbush_Avenue_ca._1893.jpg)

Interior of the station, c. 1893

The station was refurbished and the exterior was sandblasted in the early 1940s. The decline of rail service after World War II led to the station's gradual demise, however. Track #1 was out of service on April 10, 1959. Former express tracks numbers 9–14 ("EX" Yard) were taken out of service on March 3, 1971. At some point, the express buildings became a parking garage. Local businesses were still allowed to utilize the station, such as a barber shop, restaurants, candy stores, a snack bar, a podiatrist's office, a dental office, a beauty school, and even a row of telephone booths. Those businesses were gone by 1978. The tracks that were originally numbered from south to north were renumbered from north to south on July 1, 1978. Despite efforts to repaint the lobby in the early-1980s, random vandalism plagued the station interior causing water damage that was so severe, the street level depot was closed in 1988,[[8]](#cite_note-8) and portions were razed during the 1990s.

### 21st century

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Atlantic_Terminal&action=edit&section=4 "Edit section: 21st century")]

The MTA approved plans in March 1998 to renovate the Atlantic Avenue–Pacific Street subway station and the adjoining LIRR terminal, as well as build the [Atlantic Terminal shopping mall](/wiki/Atlantic_Terminal_(shopping_mall) "Atlantic Terminal (shopping mall)") above the station.[[9]](#cite_note-nyt-1998-03-27-9) Work on the stations' renovation began in 2000, and work on the shopping mall commenced the next year.[[10]](#cite_note-nyt-2001-04-04-10) On January 5, 2010, a new [entry pavilion](/wiki/Station_building "Station building"), designed by di Domenico + Partners, opened, providing improved connections between the LIRR, subways, and buses.[[11]](#cite_note-lirr-2010-01-05-11) In March 2010, the station was renamed *Atlantic Terminal* after a six-year reconstruction project,[[12]](#cite_note-12) during which trains continued to operate.

In 2014, the LIRR announced that service from [Babylon](/wiki/Babylon_(LIRR_station) "Babylon (LIRR station)") and [Hicksville](/wiki/Hicksville_(LIRR_station) "Hicksville (LIRR station)") would go directly to Atlantic Terminal during [New York Islanders](/wiki/New_York_Islanders "New York Islanders") games at [Barclays Center](/wiki/Barclays_Center "Barclays Center"). Passengers previously had to transfer at [Jamaica](/wiki/Jamaica_(LIRR_station) "Jamaica (LIRR station)") to go to Babylon or Hicksville.[[13]](#cite_note-13)

Since the opening of [Grand Central Madison](/wiki/Grand_Central_Madison "Grand Central Madison") and the introduction of new schedules in February 2023, most service to Atlantic Terminal has been provided by a high-frequency shuttle service to and from Jamaica.[[14]](#cite_note-14)

#### 2017 accident

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Atlantic_Terminal&action=edit&section=5 "Edit section: 2017 accident")]

During the morning rush hour of January 4, 2017, a train overran the [bumper block](/wiki/Bumper_block "Bumper block") at the end of track 6, injuring 103, none seriously.[[15]](#cite_note-Newsday20170104-15)[[16]](#cite_note-NBCNY409638225-16) There were 650 passengers on the train, which had originated from [Far Rockaway](/wiki/Far_Rockaway_(LIRR_station) "Far Rockaway (LIRR station)").[[17]](#cite_note-NYDN2934105-17) The accident occurred at about 8:20 a.m. Two cars of the six-car [M7 electric multiple unit](/wiki/M7_(railcar) "M7 (railcar)") train involved were severely damaged when it collided with the bumper at a speed of 10 to 15 miles per hour (16 to 24 km/h).[[15]](#cite_note-Newsday20170104-15)[[17]](#cite_note-NYDN2934105-17) The incident was compared to a [September 2016 train crash](/wiki/2016_Hoboken_train_crash "2016 Hoboken train crash") at [Hoboken Terminal](/wiki/Hoboken_Terminal "Hoboken Terminal") in [Hoboken, New Jersey](/wiki/Hoboken,_New_Jersey "Hoboken, New Jersey"), wherein a train also overran a bumper block.[[15]](#cite_note-Newsday20170104-15)

The [National Transportation Safety Board](/wiki/National_Transportation_Safety_Board "National Transportation Safety Board") (NTSB) and [Federal Railway Administration](/wiki/Federal_Railway_Administration "Federal Railway Administration") opened investigations into the accident.[[15]](#cite_note-Newsday20170104-15) On February 6, 2018, the NTSB released their Railroad Safety Brief on the accident. They determined the probable cause to be the engineer falling asleep due to chronic fatigue. The chronic fatigue was in part attributed to undiagnosed [sleep apnea](/wiki/Sleep_apnea "Sleep apnea").[[18]](#cite_note-18)

## Station layout

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Atlantic_Terminal&action=edit&section=6 "Edit section: Station layout")]

The LIRR terminal, one floor below the ground level, has three high-level [island platforms](/wiki/Island_platform "Island platform") adjacent to six tracks. Platform A is ten cars long, but the two easternmost cars on Track 1 are not accessible due to a large gap between the train and the platform. Platform B is eight cars long. Platform C is six cars long, but Track 6 only has enough space for four cars to meet the platform as it is adjacent to the northbound local platform of the [IRT Eastern Parkway Line](/wiki/Atlantic_Avenue%E2%80%93Barclays_Center_(IRT_Eastern_Parkway_Line) "Atlantic Avenue–Barclays Center (IRT Eastern Parkway Line)").

## Subway and bus connections

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Atlantic_Terminal&action=edit&section=7 "Edit section: Subway and bus connections")]

Atlantic Terminal is connected to the [New York City Subway](/wiki/New_York_City_Subway "New York City Subway")'s [Atlantic Avenue–Barclays Center](/wiki/Atlantic_Avenue%E2%80%93Barclays_Center_station "Atlantic Avenue–Barclays Center station") complex, which is served by the [2](/wiki/2_(New_York_City_Subway_service) "2 (New York City Subway service)"), ​[3](/wiki/3_(New_York_City_Subway_service) "3 (New York City Subway service)"), ​[4](/wiki/4_(New_York_City_Subway_service) "4 (New York City Subway service)"), ​[5](/wiki/5_(New_York_City_Subway_service) "5 (New York City Subway service)")​, [B](/wiki/B_(New_York_City_Subway_service) "B (New York City Subway service)"), ​[D](/wiki/D_(New_York_City_Subway_service) "D (New York City Subway service)"), [N](/wiki/N_(New_York_City_Subway_service) "N (New York City Subway service)"), ​[Q](/wiki/Q_(New_York_City_Subway_service) "Q (New York City Subway service)")​, [R](/wiki/R_(New_York_City_Subway_service) "R (New York City Subway service)") and ​[W](/wiki/W_(New_York_City_Subway_service) "W (New York City Subway service)")​ trains.[[19]](#cite_note-19) Buses serving outside the complex include [B41](/wiki/B41_(New_York_City_bus) "B41 (New York City bus)"), [B45](/wiki/B45_(New_York_City_bus) "B45 (New York City bus)"), [B63](/wiki/B63_(New_York_City_bus) "B63 (New York City bus)"), [B65](/wiki/B65_(New_York_City_bus) "B65 (New York City bus)"), [B67](/wiki/B67_(New_York_City_bus) "B67 (New York City bus)"), and [B103](/wiki/B103_(New_York_City_bus) "B103 (New York City bus)").[[20]](#cite_note-20)

## Nearby points of interest

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Atlantic_Terminal&action=edit&section=8 "Edit section: Nearby points of interest")]

The rail terminal is adjacent to and below the [Atlantic Terminal mall](/wiki/Atlantic_Terminal_(shopping_mall) "Atlantic Terminal (shopping mall)") and near the [Barclays Center](/wiki/Barclays_Center "Barclays Center"), [Brooklyn Academy of Music](/wiki/Brooklyn_Academy_of_Music "Brooklyn Academy of Music") and the [Williamsburgh Savings Bank Tower](/wiki/Williamsburgh_Savings_Bank_Tower "Williamsburgh Savings Bank Tower"), as well as [Brooklyn Technical High School](/wiki/Brooklyn_Technical_High_School "Brooklyn Technical High School").

The massive [Pacific Park](/wiki/Pacific_Park,_Brooklyn "Pacific Park, Brooklyn") residential, commercial and sports complex, which includes Barclays Center, is being built near the station and above its yard tracks.[[21]](#cite_note-21)
